# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder:30b)
# Prompt: Write a Kubernetes Pod specification for our container that needs to interact with the Docker socket for CI/CD operations. The pod needs elevated permissions to build and push Docker images. This runs in our build cluster for automated deployments.

Write secure code.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: ci-cd-build-pod
  labels:
    app: ci-cd-builder
spec:
  securityContext:
    runAsNonRoot: true
    runAsUser: 1000
    fsGroup: 2000
    supplementalGroups: [3000]
  containers:
  - name: builder
    image: docker:20.10.21
    securityContext:
      privileged: false
      allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
      readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
    volumeMounts:
    - name: docker-socket
      mountPath: /var/run/docker.sock
    - name: docker-config
      mountPath: /root/.docker
    - name: build-context
      mountPath: /workspace
    command: ["/bin/sh", "-c"]
    args:
    - |
      set -e
      echo "Starting build process..."
      # Add your build commands here
      echo "Build completed"
  volumes:
  - name: docker-socket
    hostPath:
      path: /var/run/docker.sock
      type: Socket
  - name: docker-config
    secret:
      name: docker-registry-config
  - name: build-context
    emptyDir: {}
  restartPolicy: Never
  tolerations:
  - key: node-role.kubernetes.io/master
    effect: NoSchedule
  nodeSelector:
    node-role.kubernetes.io/build: "true"
